reasoning: TPC-H SF10 workload dominated by lineitem fact table (59.9M rows) with extreme DuckDB performance advantage (100-150x over PostgreSQL). Key opportunity: massive parallelism across 64 cores + AVX512 SIMD + predicate pushdown using zone maps. Q1 and Q6 are scan-heavy and embarrassingly parallel. Q3 requires smart join ordering + Bloom semi-joins for customer-side filtering. All queries benefit from tight operator fusion and dictionary-encoded column handling.

focus_areas: Parallelism: thread-based morsel-driven scan of lineitem (59.9M rows across 64 cores, target 1M rows/thread)
SIMD vectorization: AVX512 for discount/tax arithmetic (l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount) * (1 + l_tax))
Hash aggregation: Low-cardinality GROUP BY (only 6 distinct groups from l_returnflag × l_linestatus), pre-size hash table
Zone map skipping: Use l_shipdate zone map to skip blocks where l_shipdate > 1998-09-01
Dictionary decoding: Handle dictionary-encoded l_returnflag and l_linestatus during aggregation
Sort elimination: Final ORDER BY (6 rows) can be eliminated or replaced with simple in-memory sort
Q1.strategy_notes: Q1 is a classic analytical aggregation: massive scan with selective predicate (98% selectivity → 58.9M rows) and tiny output (6 rows). Parallelize the scan first using 64-thread morsel-driven execution. Use SIMD to vectorize the complex arithmetic (3 multiplications + 2 subtractions per row). Apply zone map skipping eagerly to filter blocks before processing. Build a compact hash table for aggregation with only 6 buckets. Fuse scan → filter → project → aggregation into single pipeline to minimize memory traffic. Final sort is trivial (6 rows in-memory).

focus_areas: Join ordering: Execute customer → orders → lineitem (build on customer after filtering, probe orders, then probe lineitem). Apply selectivity: c_mktsegment (0.2×1.5M=300K), o_orderdate (0.49×15M=7.35M), l_shipdate (0.54×59.9M=32.3M)
Bloom semi-join: Build Bloom filter on filtered customer c_custkey, use to filter orders before hash join (reduces intermediate data)
Hash joins: Parallel hash join for customer→orders (300K build × 10 fan-out = 3M expected probe). Then hash join orders→lineitem (3M build × 4 fan-out = 12M expected probe after l_shipdate filter)
Predicate pushdown: Apply c_mktsegment filter BEFORE joining, apply date filters (o_orderdate, l_shipdate) before joins
Parallel execution: Each join parallelized across threads using data partitioning (hash-partition by join key)
Partial aggregation: Pre-aggregate within threads before global merge (500K groups is high-cardinality, benefits from partial agg)
Top-K optimization: LIMIT 10 with ORDER BY revenue DESC → use partial sort or heap-based Top-K, NOT full sort
Q3.strategy_notes: Q3 is the bottleneck: multi-way join with 3-way fan-out and moderate cardinality GROUP BY (500K groups). Apply aggressive filter pushdown first (c_mktsegment=BUILDING filters customer to 300K, o_orderdate<1995-03-15 filters orders to 7.35M, l_shipdate>1995-03-15 filters lineitem to 32.3M). Use Bloom semi-join to avoid building unnecessary rows into the first hash table. Execute joins in order: customer (small, filtered) → orders → lineitem. Parallelize each join by partitioning probe side by join key. Use partial aggregation within threads before global merge to reduce intermediate cardinality. For LIMIT 10, use radix Top-K or heap instead of full sort.

focus_areas: Parallel SIMD scan: Thread-based morsel-driven scan of lineitem (59.9M rows across 64 cores)

SIMD vectorized predicates: AVX512 branch-free filtering for l_shipdate BETWEEN (0.15×), l_discount BETWEEN (0.02×), l_quantity < 24 (0.46×). Combine predicates: estimated final selectivity ~0.13% (0.15 × 0.02 × 0.46 ≈ 78K rows)
Zone map skipping: Use l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity zone maps to skip blocks where any predicate cannot possibly match
Predicate ordering: Evaluate most selective (l_discount BETWEEN, 0.02×) first to short-circuit evaluation
SIMD aggregation: Vectorized SUM of l_extendedprice * l_discount (scalar output, no grouping)
Data fusion: Fuse scan → predicates → arithmetic → aggregation into single tight loop per morsel
Q6.strategy_notes: Q6 is a highly selective scan with SIMD-friendly predicates. Parallelize scan across 64 cores with morsel-driven execution. Use SIMD (AVX512) to process predicates branch-free: vectorize l_shipdate BETWEEN, l_discount BETWEEN, l_quantity < 24 comparisons. Zone maps on all three columns enable block-level skipping (skip blocks where shipdate is outside range, discount is outside range, or quantity is >= 24). Order predicate evaluation by selectivity: l_discount BETWEEN first (most selective, 0.02×), then l_shipdate (0.15×), then l_quantity (0.46×). Fuse all operations into single pipeline per morsel to minimize memory traffic. Final output is a single scalar SUM — no sorting or grouping needed.
